test_cases.description: Feature documentary — public defenders — Sundance Institute Documentary Fund, development stage
test_cases.input_summary: 90-min film, 3 subjects (2 confirmed), legal/justice system subject, development workshop already awarded, $35K development ask, fund priorities provided
test_cases.output_summary: 900-word narrative, opens on specific scene description, maps to fund's 'compelling characters + urgent social issues' criteria, credibility section names prior workshop support, closes with specific use-of-funds breakdown

test_cases.description: Short documentary — indigenous language preservation — regional cultural foundation, production stage
test_cases.input_summary: 45-min film, full access confirmed, in production, requesting completion funding, foundation priorities: cultural heritage and underrepresented communities
test_cases.output_summary: Shorter proposal (600 words) reflecting production-stage confidence, leads with cultural-heritage framing matched to funder language, use-of-funds section specific to post-production and outreach costs

test_cases.description: Series pilot — investigative journalism documentary — editorial fund with journalism-specific criteria
test_cases.input_summary: 3-part investigative series, co-produced with a print newsroom, fund priorities emphasize investigative journalism and accountability reporting
test_cases.output_summary: Proposal foregrounds newsroom partnership as credibility anchor, uses journalism-specific language (public accountability, editorial independence), flags that series structure requires additional series bible for full evaluation

notes: Consistency and professionalism are strongest — the output reads as credible professional grant writing. Actionability is the weakest dimension because the proposal always requires user verification of credentials and funder-specific formatting before actual submission.
